title: At the Café
passage:
Anna: Hello! I'd like a coffee, please.
Barista: Sure. Small or large?
Anna: Small, please.
Barista: Anything else?
Anna: No, thank you. How much is it?
Barista: That's three dollars.
Anna: Here you go. Thank you!

questions.question: What does Anna want to order?
questions.explanation: Anna says, "I'd like a coffee, please." That tells us she wants coffee, not tea, juice, or water.

questions.question: What size does Anna choose?
questions.explanation: When the barista asks "Small or large?", Anna answers "Small, please." So she chooses a small coffee.

questions.question: How much does Anna pay?
questions.explanation: The barista says, "That's three dollars." Anna then pays and thanks the barista.

title: At the Bus Stop
passage:
Tom: Excuse me, does this bus go to the city center?
Woman: Yes, it does. It comes every ten minutes.
Tom: Great. Is there a ticket machine here?
Woman: No, you can pay on the bus with a card.
Tom: Perfect. Thank you for your help!
Woman: You're welcome. Have a nice day!

questions.question: Where does Tom want to go?
questions.explanation: Tom asks if the bus goes to the city center. The woman confirms that it does.

questions.question: How often does the bus come?
questions.explanation: The woman says the bus comes every ten minutes. That means Tom does not have to wait very long.

questions.question: How can Tom pay for the bus?
options: With a card on the bus
questions.correct_answer: With a card on the bus
questions.explanation: Tom asks about a ticket machine, but the woman says there is no machine. She tells him he can pay on the bus with a card.

questions.question: What does the woman say at the end?
questions.explanation: After Tom thanks her, the woman says, "You're welcome. Have a nice day!" It is a friendly way to end the conversation.

title: Shopping for Groceries
passage:
Clerk: Good afternoon! Can I help you?
Luis: Yes, I'm looking for rice and eggs.
Clerk: The rice is in aisle three. Eggs are in aisle one, near the milk.
Luis: Thank you. Do you have brown rice?
Clerk: Yes, we do. It's on the top shelf.
Luis: Great. I'll take a bag of brown rice and a dozen eggs.
Clerk: Anything else today?
Luis: No, that's all. How much will that be?
Clerk: Twelve dollars and fifty cents.
Luis: Here is my card.

questions.question: What is Luis looking for first?
questions.explanation: Luis tells the clerk he is looking for rice and eggs. Those are the first items he mentions.

questions.question: In which aisle are the eggs?
questions.explanation: The clerk says eggs are in aisle one, near the milk. Luis goes there after learning where the rice is.

questions.question: Where is the brown rice?
options: In aisle three on the top shelf
At the front of the store
questions.correct_answer: In aisle three on the top shelf
questions.explanation: The clerk says rice is in aisle three and that brown rice is on the top shelf. Luis decides to buy a bag of it.

questions.question: How many eggs does Luis buy?
questions.explanation: Luis says he will take a dozen eggs. A dozen means twelve eggs.

questions.question: How much does Luis pay?
questions.explanation: The clerk tells Luis the total is twelve dollars and fifty cents. Luis then pays with his card.

title: Meeting a New Neighbor
passage:
Sara: Hi! I'm Sara. I live in apartment four.
Ben: Nice to meet you, Sara. I'm Ben from apartment five.
Sara: Welcome to the building! How long have you lived here?
Ben: Just one week. I moved from another city.
Sara: If you need anything, just knock on my door.
Ben: That's very kind. Thank you!

questions.question: Which apartment does Sara live in?
questions.explanation: Sara introduces herself and says she lives in apartment four. Ben lives next door in apartment five.

questions.question: How long has Ben lived in the building?
questions.explanation: Ben says he has lived there for just one week. He recently moved from another city.

questions.question: What does Sara offer to do?
questions.explanation: Sara says, "If you need anything, just knock on my door." She is offering friendly help to her new neighbor.

title: Calling the Doctor
passage:
Receptionist: Good morning, City Clinic. How can I help you?
Maria: Hello. I'd like to make an appointment, please.
Receptionist: Of course. What is your name?
Maria: Maria Lopez.
Receptionist: Thank you, Maria. Is tomorrow at two o'clock okay?
Maria: Yes, that works for me.
Receptionist: Great. Please arrive ten minutes early.
Maria: I will. Thank you!

questions.question: Why does Maria call the clinic?
questions.explanation: Maria says she would like to make an appointment. The receptionist then asks for her name and offers a time.

questions.question: What is Maria's last name?
questions.explanation: Maria gives her full name as Maria Lopez. Lopez is her family name, also called a last name.

questions.question: When is Maria's appointment?
questions.explanation: The receptionist asks if tomorrow at two o'clock is okay, and Maria agrees. That is when her appointment is scheduled.

questions.question: How early should Maria arrive?
questions.explanation: The receptionist asks Maria to arrive ten minutes early. Maria says she will do that before her appointment.
